what_it_does: TypeScript declaration file for a React form-field component named Input. Declares the InputProps interface (extending React.InputHTMLAttributes<HTMLInputElement>) and exports the Input function component plus a default export.
observations: Adds four props on top of the standard input attributes: as ('input' | 'textarea' | 'select', default 'input'), label, hint, and error (all React.ReactNode).
Docstring states a text input is rendered by default with a 'gold focus ring from the source composer'.
Providing label/hint/error wraps the control in a labelled .cv-field stack; omitting them yields a bare control.
error is documented to set aria-invalid and apply 'reject' styling.
standouts: Single component covers input/textarea/select via the as prop rather than three components.
Accessibility built into the contract: error sets aria-invalid.
conventions: Polymorphic 'as' prop pattern for choosing the rendered element.
References shared CSS classes (.cv-field) and tokens (gold focus ring) rather than inline styling — consistent with the design-system single-source model.
JSDoc on the interface and each prop.
notes: The .d.ts contract for the ConceptV form-field Input — one polymorphic control (input/textarea/select) with optional label/hint/error that triggers a labelled .cv-field wrapper.
questions: What is 'the source composer' that provides the gold focus ring (referenced, not defined here)?
How are textarea/select-specific attributes typed given the interface extends only InputHTMLAttributes<HTMLInputElement>?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — the docstring describes a polymorphic labelled form field and the interface declares exactly those props.
apparent_themes: Polymorphic form control (one component, three element kinds)
intention_signals: Aims to provide one typed, accessible form-field abstraction that can render any of the three control kinds and optionally self-wrap with label/hint/error.
contribution: Contributes the typed contract for the unified form field — the as union plus the label/hint/error props that drive the .cv-field wrapper and reject/aria-invalid styling.
summary_for_embedding: TypeScript .d.ts declaring InputProps and the Input React component, a ConceptV form field that renders input, textarea, or select with optional label, hint, and error that trigger a labelled .cv-field wrapper and aria-invalid styling.
intention_for_embedding: To define the typed contract for a single polymorphic, accessible form-field component in the design system.
